docs.title: m9m Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for m9m, a high-performance workflow automation platform built in Go.

docs.title: What is m9m?
docs.text: m9m is a cloud-native workflow automation platform that delivers enterprise-grade performance while maintaining compatibility with existing n8n workflows. Built from the ground up in Go, m9m offers significant performance improvements over Node.js-based alternatives.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• High Performance

    5-10x faster workflow execution with 70% lower memory usage

  • Cloud Native

    Designed for Kubernetes with horizontal scaling support

  • 100+ Integrations

    Support for databases, APIs, cloud platforms, and AI services

  • Enterprise Ready

    Built-in monitoring, tracing, and security features

docs.title: Performance Comparison
docs.text: Metric n8n m9m Improvement Workflow Execution 500ms 100ms 5x faster Memory Usage 512MB 150MB 70% reduction Cold Start Time 3s 500ms 6x faster Concurrent Workflows 50 500 10x scale Container Size 1.2GB 300MB 75% smaller

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Get up and running in minutes:

DockerBinaryFrom Source 
docker run -p 8080:8080 m9m/m9m:latest

wget https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-linux-amd64
chmod +x m9m-linux-amd64
./m9m-linux-amd64 serve

git clone https://github.com/m9m/m9m.git
cd m9m
make build
./m9m serve

Then open http://localhost:8080 in your browser.

docs.title: API Authentication
docs.text: Configure authentication for the m9m REST API.

docs.title: Authentication Methods
docs.text:
m9m supports multiple authentication methods:

  1. API Key - Simple key-based authentication
  2. Bearer Token - JWT token authentication
  3. Basic Auth - Username/password (development only)
  4. OAuth2 - Third-party OAuth providers

docs.title: Generate API Key
docs.text:
m9m apikey create --name "My Application"

Output: 

API Key created successfully
Name: My Application
Key: m9m_sk_abc123def456...
Created: 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z

Save this key securely - it cannot be retrieved again.

docs.title: Use API Key
docs.text:
Include in request header:

curl -H "X-API-Key: m9m_sk_abc123def456..." \
  http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

docs.title: Manage API Keys
docs.text:
# List API keys
m9m apikey list

# Revoke API key
m9m apikey revoke m9m_sk_abc123def456

# Rotate API key
m9m apikey rotate m9m_sk_abc123def456

docs.title: API Key Scopes
docs.text:
Create keys with limited permissions:

m9m apikey create --name "Read Only" --scopes "read:workflows,read:executions"

Available scopes: | Scope | Description | |-------|-------------| | read:workflows | Read workflow data | | write:workflows | Create/update workflows | | execute:workflows | Execute workflows | | read:executions | Read execution data | | read:credentials | Read credential metadata | | write:credentials | Create/update credentials | | admin | Full access |

docs.title: Generate Token
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/auth/token
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "username": "admin",
  "password": "password"
}

Response: 

{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...",
    "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...",
    "expiresIn": 3600
  }
}

docs.title: Use Bearer Token
docs.text:
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..." \
  http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

docs.title: Refresh Token
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."
}

docs.title: Token Configuration
docs.text:
# config.yaml
auth:
  jwt:
    secret: "your-secret-key"
    accessTokenExpiry: 1h
    refreshTokenExpiry: 7d
    issuer: "m9m"

docs.title: Basic Authentication
docs.text:
Development Only

Basic authentication should only be used in development environments.

curl -u admin:password \
  http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

Enable in configuration:

auth:
  basicAuth:
    enabled: true
    users:
      - username: admin
        password: $2a$10$... # bcrypt hash

docs.title: Configure OAuth Provider
docs.text:
auth:
  oauth2:
    enabled: true
    providers:
      github:
        clientId: "your-client-id"
        clientSecret: "your-client-secret"
        scopes: ["user:email"]
      google:
        clientId: "your-client-id"
        clientSecret: "your-client-secret"
        scopes: ["email", "profile"]

docs.title: OAuth Flow
docs.text:
1. Redirect user to: 

    GET /api/v1/auth/oauth2/github

  2. User authenticates with provider

  3. Callback returns tokens: 

    GET /api/v1/auth/oauth2/callback?code=...

docs.text: For machine-to-machine authentication:

docs.title: Go
docs.text:
import "github.com/m9m/m9m-go-sdk"

// API Key
client := m9m.NewClient(
    m9m.WithBaseURL("http://localhost:8080"),
    m9m.WithAPIKey("m9m_sk_abc123"),
)

// Bearer Token
client := m9m.NewClient(
    m9m.WithBaseURL("http://localhost:8080"),
    m9m.WithBearerToken("eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."),
)

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import m9m

# API Key
client = m9m.Client(
    base_url="http://localhost:8080",
    api_key="m9m_sk_abc123"

# Bearer Token
client = m9m.Client(
    base_url="http://localhost:8080",
    token="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."
)

docs.title: JavaScript
docs.text:
const { M9MClient } = require('@m9m/sdk');

// API Key
const client = new M9MClient({
  baseUrl: 'http://localhost:8080',

// Bearer Token

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use API keys for server-to-server communication
  2. Use OAuth2 for user authentication
  3. Rotate keys regularly (every 90 days)
  4. Use scoped permissions - least privilege
  5. Store secrets securely - never in code
  6. Enable HTTPS in production
  7. Monitor authentication failures

docs.title: REST API
docs.text: m9m provides a comprehensive REST API for managing workflows, executions, and credentials.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: http://localhost:8080/api/v1

docs.title: API Key
docs.text:
Include API key in header:

curl -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

docs.title: Bearer Token
docs.text:
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer your-token" \
  http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
All responses are JSON:

{
  "success": true,
  "data": {},
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 100
  }
}

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Invalid workflow definition",
    "details": {
      "field": "nodes",
      "reason": "At least one node is required"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: List Workflows
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/workflows

Query parameters: | Parameter | Type | Description | |-----------|------|-------------| | page | int | Page number (default: 1) | | limit | int | Items per page (default: 20, max: 100) | | active | bool | Filter by active status | | search | string | Search in name/description |

Response: 

{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "wf-001",
      "name": "My Workflow",
      "active": true,
      "createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
      "updatedAt": "2024-01-15T12:30:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 45
  }
}

docs.text:
GET /api/v1/workflows/:id

{
  "success": true,

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text: Webhooks allow external services to trigger m9m workflows via HTTP requests.

docs.title: Production Webhooks
docs.text: https://your-m9m-host/webhook/<path>

docs.title: Test Webhooks
docs.text:
For development and testing:

https://your-m9m-host/webhook-test/<path>

Test webhooks don't require the workflow to be active.

docs.title: In Workflow
docs.text:
Add a Webhook node:

{
  "id": "webhook-trigger",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
  "parameters": {
    "path": "my-webhook",
    "httpMethod": "POST",
    "responseMode": "onReceived"
  }
}

docs.title: Webhook Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description path URL path (e.g., my-webhook → /webhook/my-webhook) httpMethod HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) responseMode When to respond (see below) responseData What to return

docs.title: Immediately (onReceived)
docs.text:
Respond immediately upon receiving the request:

{
  "parameters": {
    "responseMode": "onReceived",
    "responseCode": 202,
    "responseData": "allEntries"
  }
}

docs.title: After Workflow (lastNode)
docs.text:
Wait for workflow to complete:

{
  "parameters": {
    "responseMode": "lastNode"
  }
}

docs.title: Custom Response (responseNode)
docs.text:
Use a Respond to Webhook node:

{
  "id": "respond",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",
  "parameters": {
    "respondWith": "json",
    "responseBody": {
      "success": true,
      "id": "={{ $json.createdId }}"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Request Validation

Always validate incoming requests:

// Validate required fields
if (!$json.body.orderId) {

// Validate data types

  throw new Error('amount must be a number');

docs.title: Idempotency
docs.text:
Handle duplicate webhook deliveries:

const eventId = $json.headers['x-event-id'];

// Check if already processed
const processed = await redis.get(`event:${eventId}`);
if (processed) {
  return { skipped: true, reason: 'duplicate' };
}

// Mark as processed
await redis.set(`event:${eventId}`, '1', 'EX', 86400);

// Process event
return processEvent($json.body);

docs.title: Basic Setup
docs.text:
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  m9m:
    image: m9m/m9m:latest
    container_name: m9m
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "9090:9090"
    environment:
      - M9M_DB_TYPE=postgres
      - M9M_DB_URL=postgres://m9m:password@postgres/m9m
      - M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: m9m-postgres
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=m9m
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password
      - POSTGRES_DB=m9m
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U m9m"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postgres_data:

Start: 

docker compose up -d

docs.title: Production Setup
docs.text:
version: '3.8'

services:
  m9m:
    image: m9m/m9m:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "9090:9090"
    environment:
      - M9M_DB_TYPE=postgres
      - M9M_DB_URL=postgres://m9m:${DB_PASSWORD}@postgres/m9m?sslmode=require
      - M9M_QUEUE_TYPE=redis
      - M9M_QUEUE_URL=redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@redis:6379
      - M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
      - M9M_MAX_WORKERS=20
      - M9M_LOG_LEVEL=info
    volumes:
      - ./config:/app/config:ro
      - ./data:/data
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '2'
          memory: 2G
        reservations:
          cpus: '0.5'
          memory: 512M
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8080/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "3"

  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=m9m
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_DB=m9m
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U m9m"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '1'
          memory: 1G
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD} --appendonly yes
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "-a", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '0.5'
          memory: 512M
    restart: unless-stopped

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./certs:/etc/nginx/certs:ro
    depends_on:
      - m9m
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:

docs.title: Environment File
docs.text:
# .env
DB_PASSWORD=your-secure-db-password
REDIS_PASSWORD=your-secure-redis-password
ENCRYPTION_KEY=your-32-character-encryption-key

docs.title: Nginx Configuration
docs.text:
# nginx.conf
events {
    worker_connections 1024;
}

http {
    upstream m9m {
        server m9m:8080;
    }

    server {
        listen 80;
        server_name your-domain.com;
        return 301 https://$server_name$request_uri;
    }

    server {
        listen 443 ssl http2;
        server_name your-domain.com;

        ssl_certificate /etc/nginx/certs/cert.pem;
        ssl_certificate_key /etc/nginx/certs/key.pem;

        location / {
            proxy_pass http://m9m;
            proxy_http_version 1.1;
            proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
            proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
            proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
        }

        location /webhook {
            proxy_pass http://m9m;
            proxy_http_version 1.1;
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
            proxy_read_timeout 120s;
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Backup
docs.text:
# Backup database
docker exec m9m-postgres pg_dump -U m9m m9m > backup.sql

# Backup volumes
docker run --rm \
  -v m9m_postgres_data:/data \
  -v $(pwd):/backup \
  alpine tar czf /backup/postgres-backup.tar.gz /data

docs.title: Restore
docs.text:
# Restore database
docker exec -i m9m-postgres psql -U m9m m9m < backup.sql

# Restore volumes
docker run --rm \
  -v m9m_postgres_data:/data \
  -v $(pwd):/backup \
  alpine tar xzf /backup/postgres-backup.tar.gz -C /

docs.title: Kubernetes Deployment
docs.text: Deploy m9m on Kubernetes for production-grade scalability.

docs.text:
• Kubernetes cluster (1.24+)

docs.title: Basic Deployment
docs.text:
# m9m-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: m9m
  labels:
    app: m9m
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: m9m
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: m9m
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: m9m
        image: m9m/m9m:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9090
          name: metrics
        env:
        - name: M9M_DB_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: database-url
        - name: M9M_QUEUE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: redis-url
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 250m
            memory: 256Mi
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 10
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 20

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres

spec:

  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:

  template:
    metadata:

    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5432
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: m9m
        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: db-password
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: m9m
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1Gi
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
---

kind: Service

  namespace: m9m
spec:
  ports:

  selector:

docs.title: Full m9m Deployment
docs.text:
# m9m-full.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: m9m
  namespace: m9m
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: m9m
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: m9m
      annotations:
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        prometheus.io/port: "9090"
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: m9m
      containers:
      - name: m9m
        image: m9m/m9m:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9090
          name: metrics
        env:
        - name: M9M_DB_TYPE
          value: postgres
        - name: M9M_DB_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: database-url
        - name: M9M_QUEUE_TYPE
          value: redis
        - name: M9M_QUEUE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: redis-url
        - name: M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: m9m-secrets
              key: encryption-key
        volumeMounts:
        - name: config
          mountPath: /app/config
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "2"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 10
          failureThreshold: 3
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 20
          failureThreshold: 3
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: m9m-config
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - weight: 100
            podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  app: m9m
              topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• Use PostgreSQL for database
  • Use Redis or RabbitMQ for queue

docs.title: Encryption Key
docs.text:
Generate a strong encryption key:

openssl rand -hex 16

Set as environment variable: 

export M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY="your-32-character-key"

docs.title: HTTPS
docs.text:
Always use HTTPS in production:

# nginx.conf
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name m9m.example.com;

    ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/cert.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/key.pem;
    ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
    ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256;
    ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://m9m:8080;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;
    }
}

docs.title: Backups
docs.text:
Automated daily backups:

#!/bin/bash
# backup.sh
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
BACKUP_DIR="/backups"

# Database backup
pg_dump -h postgres -U m9m m9m | gzip > "$BACKUP_DIR/db_$DATE.sql.gz"

# Upload to S3
aws s3 cp "$BACKUP_DIR/db_$DATE.sql.gz" s3://m9m-backups/db/

# Cleanup old backups (keep 30 days)
find $BACKUP_DIR -type f -mtime +30 -delete

docs.title: Prometheus Metrics
docs.text:
Key metrics to monitor:

Metric Alert Threshold m9m_workflow_executions_total Track trends m9m_workflow_errors_total > 1% error rate m9m_execution_duration_seconds p99 > 30s m9m_queue_size > 1000 pending m9m_active_workflows Capacity planning

docs.title: Alerting Rules
docs.text:
# alerts.yaml
groups:
- name: m9m
  rules:
  - alert: HighErrorRate
    expr: rate(m9m_workflow_errors_total[5m]) / rate(m9m_workflow_executions_total[5m]) > 0.01
    for: 5m
    labels:
      severity: critical
    annotations:
      summary: High workflow error rate

  - alert: QueueBacklog
    expr: m9m_queue_size > 1000
    for: 10m
    labels:
      severity: warning
    annotations:
      summary: Queue backlog growing

  - alert: HighLatency
    expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, m9m_execution_duration_seconds_bucket) > 30
    for: 5m
    labels:
      severity: warning
    annotations:
      summary: High workflow execution latency

docs.title: Your First Workflow
docs.text: Build a complete workflow that monitors a website and sends notifications.

docs.title: What We're Building
docs.text:
A workflow that:

  1. Runs on a schedule (every 5 minutes)
  2. Checks if a website is responding
  3. Sends a Slack notification if the site is down

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• m9m installed and running
  • Slack webhook URL (for notifications)

docs.title: Step 1: Create the Workflow Structure
docs.text:
Create a file site-monitor.json:

{
  "name": "Site Monitor",
  "active": true,
  "nodes": [],
  "connections": {}
}

docs.title: Step 2: Add the Trigger Node
docs.text:
Add a cron trigger that runs every 5 minutes:

{
  "id": "cron",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.cron",
  "position": [250, 300],
  "parameters": {
    "triggerTimes": {
      "item": [
        {
          "mode": "everyMinute",
          "minute": 5
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Step 3: Add the HTTP Request Node
docs.text:
Check the target website:

{
  "id": "check-site",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
  "position": [450, 300],
  "parameters": {
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "method": "GET",
    "timeout": 10000,
    "options": {
      "response": {
        "response": {
          "neverError": true
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

neverError Option

Setting neverError: true prevents the workflow from stopping on HTTP errors. We want to capture the error and handle it ourselves.

docs.title: Step 4: Add the Filter Node
docs.text:
Filter for failed responses:

{
  "id": "filter",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.filter",
  "position": [650, 300],
  "parameters": {
    "conditions": {
      "number": [
        {
          "value1": "={{ $json.statusCode }}",
          "operation": "notEqual",
          "value2": 200
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Step 5: Add the Notification Node
docs.text:
Send a Slack alert when the site is down:

{
  "id": "notify",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.slack",
  "position": [850, 300],
  "parameters": {
    "resource": "message",
    "operation": "post",
    "channel": "#alerts",
    "text": "Site is DOWN! Status: {{ $json.statusCode }}",
    "attachments": [
      {
        "color": "danger",
        "fields": [
          {
            "title": "URL",
            "value": "https://example.com"
          },
          {
            "title": "Status Code",
            "value": "={{ $json.statusCode }}"
          },
          {
            "title": "Time",
            "value": "={{ $now.format('YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss') }}"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "credentials": {
    "slackApi": {
      "id": "1",
      "name": "Slack Account"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Step 6: Connect the Nodes
docs.text:
Add connections between nodes:

{
  "connections": {
    "cron": {
      "main": [[{"node": "check-site", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "check-site": {
      "main": [[{"node": "filter", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "filter": {
      "main": [[{"node": "notify", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Complete Workflow
docs.text:
Here's the complete workflow:

{
  "name": "Site Monitor",
  "active": true,
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "cron",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.cron",
      "position": [250, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "triggerTimes": {
          "item": [
            {
              "mode": "everyMinute",
              "minute": 5
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "check-site",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "position": [450, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "url": "https://example.com",
        "method": "GET",
        "timeout": 10000,
        "options": {
          "response": {
            "response": {
              "neverError": true
            }
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "filter",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.filter",
      "position": [650, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "number": [
            {
              "value1": "={{ $json.statusCode }}",
              "operation": "notEqual",
              "value2": 200
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "notify",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.slack",
      "position": [850, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "message",
        "operation": "post",
        "channel": "#alerts",
        "text": "Site is DOWN! Status: {{ $json.statusCode }}"
      },
      "credentials": {
        "slackApi": {
          "id": "1",
          "name": "Slack Account"
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "cron": {
      "main": [[{"node": "check-site", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "check-site": {
      "main": [[{"node": "filter", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "filter": {
      "main": [[{"node": "notify", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Set Up Credentials
docs.text:
Before running, configure Slack credentials:

# Via CLI
m9m credentials create slack \
  --type slackApi \
  --data '{"accessToken": "xoxb-your-token"}'

Or via the Web UI:

  1. Go to Settings → Credentials
  2. Click Add Credential
  3. Select Slack API
  4. Enter your access token
  5. Click Save

docs.title: Option 1: Register with Server
docs.text:
m9m workflow register site-monitor.json

This registers the workflow with the server and starts the cron trigger.

docs.title: Option 2: Import via API
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @site-monitor.json

docs.title: Option 3: Test Manually
docs.text:
Test the workflow without the cron trigger:

m9m execute site-monitor.json --skip-trigger

docs.title: Monitor Execution
docs.text:
View workflow executions:

# List recent executions
m9m executions list --workflow "Site Monitor"

# View execution details
m9m executions get <execution-id>

# View logs
m9m logs --workflow "Site Monitor" --tail

docs.title: Add Success Notification
docs.text:
Add a parallel branch for successful checks:

{
  "id": "filter-success",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.filter",
  "parameters": {
    "conditions": {
      "number": [
        {
          "value1": "={{ $json.statusCode }}",
          "operation": "equal",
          "value2": 200
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Add Response Time Tracking
docs.text:
Include response time in notifications:

{
  "text": "Site DOWN! Status: {{ $json.statusCode }}, Response Time: {{ $json.responseTime }}ms"
}

docs.title: Add Multiple Sites
docs.text:
Monitor multiple sites with a loop:

{
  "id": "sites",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
  "parameters": {
    "values": {
      "array": [
        {
          "name": "sites",
          "value": ["https://example.com", "https://api.example.com"]
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide covers all the ways to install m9m on your system.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Component Minimum Recommended CPU 1 core 2+ cores Memory 256MB 512MB+ Disk 500MB 1GB+ OS Linux, macOS, Windows Linux

docs.title: Docker (Recommended)
docs.text:
The easiest way to run m9m is with Docker:

docker run -d \
  --name m9m \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -p 9090:9090 \
  -v m9m_data:/data \
  m9m/m9m:latest

Docker Compose

For production deployments, see the Docker deployment guide for a complete Docker Compose setup.

docs.title: Binary Download
docs.text:
Download pre-built binaries for your platform:

Linux (amd64)Linux (arm64)macOS (amd64)macOS (arm64)Windows 
# Download
wget https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-linux-amd64

# Make executable
chmod +x m9m-linux-amd64

# Move to PATH
sudo mv m9m-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/m9m

# Verify installation
m9m --version

wget https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-linux-arm64
chmod +x m9m-linux-arm64
sudo mv m9m-linux-arm64 /usr/local/bin/m9m
m9m --version

wget https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-darwin-amd64
chmod +x m9m-darwin-amd64
sudo mv m9m-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/m9m
m9m --version

wget https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-darwin-arm64
chmod +x m9m-darwin-arm64
sudo mv m9m-darwin-arm64 /usr/local/bin/m9m
m9m --version

# Download
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://github.com/m9m/m9m/releases/latest/download/m9m-windows-amd64.exe" -OutFile "m9m.exe"

# Add to PATH or run directly
.\m9m.exe --version

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
Build m9m from source code:

# Prerequisites: Go 1.21+
go version

# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/m9m/m9m.git
cd m9m

# Install dependencies
go mod download

# Build
make build

# Verify
./m9m --version

docs.title: Development Build
docs.text:
For development with additional tools:

# Install development dependencies
make deps

# Build with race detector
go build -race -o m9m cmd/m9m/main.go

# Run tests
make test

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
After installation, verify m9m is working:

# Check version
m9m --version

# View help
m9m --help

# Start server
m9m serve

You should see output like:

m9m v1.0.0
Build: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
Go: go1.21.5

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Configure m9m using environment variables:

# Server settings
export M9M_PORT=8080
export M9M_HOST=0.0.0.0

# Queue settings
export M9M_QUEUE_TYPE=redis
export M9M_QUEUE_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# Monitoring
export M9M_METRICS_PORT=9090

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Create a configuration file at ~/.m9m/config.yaml:

server:
  port: 8080
  host: "0.0.0.0"

queue:
  type: "memory"
  max_workers: 10

monitoring:
  metrics_port: 9090
  enable_tracing: false

database:
  type: "sqlite"
  path: "~/.m9m/data.db"

docs.title: Systemd Service (Linux)
docs.text:
Run m9m as a systemd service:

# /etc/systemd/system/m9m.service
[Unit]
Description=m9m Workflow Automation
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=m9m
Group=m9m
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/m9m serve
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
Environment=M9M_PORT=8080

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Enable and start:

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable m9m
sudo systemctl start m9m
sudo systemctl status m9m

docs.title: What is m9m?
docs.text: m9m is a high-performance, cloud-native workflow automation platform built in Go. It enables you to automate tasks, integrate services, and build complex workflows with minimal resource usage and maximum performance.

docs.title: Performance First
docs.text:
m9m was built from the ground up in Go to deliver exceptional performance:

  • 5-10x faster workflow execution compared to Node.js alternatives
  • 70% lower memory footprint (~150MB vs 512MB)
  • Sub-second startup times (500ms vs 3s)
  • 75% smaller container images (300MB vs 1.2GB)

docs.title: Cloud Native
docs.text:
Designed for modern infrastructure:

  • Kubernetes-native deployment
  • Horizontal scaling with Redis/RabbitMQ queues
  • Prometheus metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing
  • Health checks and graceful shutdown

docs.title: n8n Compatible
docs.text:
Migrate without friction:

  • 95% workflow compatibility with n8n
  • Same expression syntax and functions
  • Compatible credential management
  • Easy workflow import/export

docs.title: Extensive Integrations
docs.text:
Connect to 100+ services:

  • Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch
  • Cloud: AWS, GCP, Azure
  • Messaging: Slack, Discord, Telegram, Microsoft Teams
  • AI/LLM: OpenAI, Anthropic, local models
  • Version Control: GitHub, GitLab
  • Productivity: Google Sheets, Microsoft 365

docs.title: Data Integration
docs.text:
Connect and synchronize data between different systems:

CRM → Transform → Database → Notification

docs.title: Workflow Automation
docs.text:
Automate repetitive business processes:

Form Submission → Validation → Database → Email → Slack

docs.title: Event Processing
docs.text:
React to events from various sources:

Webhook → Process → Multiple Destinations

docs.title: AI Pipelines
docs.text:
Build intelligent automation with AI:

Input → LLM Analysis → Decision → Action

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: A workflow is a collection of nodes connected together to perform a task. Workflows define the logic of your automation.

docs.title: Nodes
docs.text:
Nodes are the building blocks of workflows. Each node performs a specific action:

  • Trigger Nodes: Start workflow execution (webhooks, schedules, events)
  • Action Nodes: Perform operations (HTTP requests, database queries)
  • Transform Nodes: Manipulate data (filter, map, merge)
  • AI Nodes: Integrate with LLMs and AI services

docs.title: Expressions
docs.text:
Expressions allow you to dynamically reference data within workflows:

{{ $json.customer.name }}
{{ $input.item.email }}
{{ $now.format('YYYY-MM-DD') }}

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text: Credentials securely store authentication information for external services. m9m encrypts credentials at rest and provides fine-grained access control.

docs.text:

  1. Install m9m on your system
  2. Follow the Quick Start guide
  3. Build Your First Workflow

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get m9m running and execute your first workflow in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Start the Server
docs.text:
Start m9m with default settings:

m9m serve

You should see:

INFO  Starting m9m server
INFO  Server listening on http://0.0.0.0:8080
INFO  Metrics available at http://0.0.0.0:9090/metrics
INFO  Ready to execute workflows

docs.title: Create a Simple Workflow
docs.text:
Create a file called hello-world.json:

{
  "name": "Hello World",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "start",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.start",
      "position": [250, 300],
      "parameters": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "set",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "position": [450, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "values": {
          "string": [
            {
              "name": "message",
              "value": "Hello from m9m!"
            },
            {
              "name": "timestamp",
              "value": "={{ $now.toISO() }}"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "start": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "set",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Via CLI
docs.text:
Execute directly from command line:

m9m execute hello-world.json

Output:

{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "message": "Hello from m9m!",
      "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "executionTime": "12ms"
}

docs.title: Via REST API
docs.text:
Execute via the API:

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows/execute \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @hello-world.json

docs.title: A More Practical Example
docs.text:
Create a workflow that fetches data from an API:

{
  "name": "API Fetch Example",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "start",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.start",
      "position": [250, 300],
      "parameters": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "http",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "position": [450, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "url": "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1",
        "method": "GET"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "transform",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "position": [650, 300],
      "parameters": {
        "values": {
          "string": [
            {
              "name": "title",
              "value": "={{ $json.title }}"
            },
            {
              "name": "summary",
              "value": "Post by user {{ $json.userId }}"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "start": {
      "main": [[{"node": "http", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "http": {
      "main": [[{"node": "transform", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    }
  }
}

Execute it:

m9m execute api-fetch.json

docs.title: View Metrics

Access Prometheus metrics:

Key metrics include:

  • m9m_workflow_executions_total - Total workflow executions
  • m9m_workflow_execution_duration_seconds - Execution time histogram
  • m9m_node_executions_total - Per-node execution counts
  • m9m_queue_size - Current queue depth

docs.title: Streaming (Code Node)
docs.text:
const response = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Authorization': `Bearer ${$env.OPENAI_API_KEY}`,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    model: 'gpt-4',
    messages: $json.messages,
    stream: true
  })
});

let fullResponse = '';
for await (const chunk of response.body) {
  const text = new TextDecoder().decode(chunk);
  // Process streaming chunks
  fullResponse += text;
}

return { json: { response: fullResponse } };

docs.title: Conversation History
docs.text:
Maintain context across messages:

{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "get-history",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.redis",
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "get",
        "key": "conversation:{{ $json.userId }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "ai-chat",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.openai",
      "parameters": {
        "messages": "={{ JSON.parse($json.history || '[]').concat([{role: 'user', content: $json.message}]) }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "save-history",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.redis",
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "set",
        "key": "conversation:{{ $json.userId }}",
        "value": "={{ JSON.stringify($json.messages) }}",
        "expire": 3600
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Token Counting
docs.text:
// Approximate token count
const tokenCount = Math.ceil($json.text.length / 4);

if (tokenCount > 4000) {
  // Split into chunks
  const chunks = splitIntoChunks($json.text, 4000);
  return chunks.map(chunk => ({ json: { text: chunk } }));
}

docs.title: Node Structure
docs.text:
Every node implements the NodeExecutor interface:

type NodeExecutor interface {
    Execute(inputData []model.DataItem, nodeParams map[string]interface{}) ([]model.DataItem, error)
    Description() NodeDescription
    ValidateParameters(params map[string]interface{}) error
}

docs.title: Basic Node Template
docs.text:
package custom

import (
    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/nodes/base"
    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/model"
)

type MyCustomNode struct {
    *base.BaseNode
}

func NewMyCustomNode() *MyCustomNode {
    return &MyCustomNode{
        BaseNode: base.NewBaseNode(base.NodeDescription{
            Name:        "My Custom Node",
            Description: "Performs custom processing",
            Category:    "custom",
        }),
    }
}

func (n *MyCustomNode) Execute(inputData []model.DataItem, nodeParams map[string]interface{}) ([]model.DataItem, error) {
    results := make([]model.DataItem, 0)

    for _, item := range inputData {
        // Process each item
        processed := model.DataItem{
            JSON: map[string]interface{}{
                "original": item.JSON,
                "processed": true,
            },
        }
        results = append(results, processed)
    }

    return results, nil
}

func (n *MyCustomNode) ValidateParameters(params map[string]interface{}) error {
    // Add validation logic
    return nil
}

docs.title: HTTP Integration Node
docs.text:
Example node that calls an external API:

package custom

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "net/http"
    "time"

    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/nodes/base"
    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/model"
)

type APIClientNode struct {
    *base.BaseNode
    client *http.Client
}

func NewAPIClientNode() *APIClientNode {
    return &APIClientNode{
        BaseNode: base.NewBaseNode(base.NodeDescription{
            Name:        "API Client",
            Description: "Calls external API",
            Category:    "http",
        }),
        client: &http.Client{
            Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
        },
    }
}

func (n *APIClientNode) Execute(inputData []model.DataItem, nodeParams map[string]interface{}) ([]model.DataItem, error) {
    url := n.GetStringParameter(nodeParams, "url", "")
    if url == "" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("url is required")
    }

    results := make([]model.DataItem, 0)

    for _, item := range inputData {
        // Build request
        req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", url, nil)
        if err != nil {
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
        }

        // Add headers
        req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")

        // Make request
        resp, err := n.client.Do(req)
        if err != nil {
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("request failed: %w", err)
        }
        defer resp.Body.Close()

        // Parse response
        var data map[string]interface{}
        if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse response: %w", err)
        }

        results = append(results, model.DataItem{
            JSON: data,
        })
    }

    return results, nil
}

func (n *APIClientNode) ValidateParameters(params map[string]interface{}) error {
    url := n.GetStringParameter(params, "url", "")
    if url == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("url parameter is required")
    }
    return nil
}

docs.title: Database Node
docs.text:
Example database integration:

package custom

import (
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/nodes/base"
    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/model"
    _ "github.com/lib/pq"
)

type DatabaseNode struct {
    *base.BaseNode
}

func NewDatabaseNode() *DatabaseNode {
    return &DatabaseNode{
        BaseNode: base.NewBaseNode(base.NodeDescription{
            Name:        "Database Query",
            Description: "Execute database queries",
            Category:    "database",
        }),
    }
}

func (n *DatabaseNode) Execute(inputData []model.DataItem, nodeParams map[string]interface{}) ([]model.DataItem, error) {
    connectionString := n.GetStringParameter(nodeParams, "connectionString", "")
    query := n.GetStringParameter(nodeParams, "query", "")

    db, err := sql.Open("postgres", connectionString)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to connect: %w", err)
    }
    defer db.Close()

    rows, err := db.Query(query)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("query failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer rows.Close()

    columns, _ := rows.Columns()
    results := make([]model.DataItem, 0)

    for rows.Next() {
        values := make([]interface{}, len(columns))
        valuePtrs := make([]interface{}, len(columns))
        for i := range values {
            valuePtrs[i] = &values[i]
        }

        rows.Scan(valuePtrs...)

        row := make(map[string]interface{})
        for i, col := range columns {
            row[col] = values[i]
        }

        results = append(results, model.DataItem{JSON: row})
    }

    return results, nil
}

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Implement proper error handling:

func (n *MyNode) Execute(inputData []model.DataItem, nodeParams map[string]interface{}) ([]model.DataItem, error) {
    results := make([]model.DataItem, 0)

    for i, item := range inputData {
        result, err := n.processItem(item, nodeParams)
        if err != nil {
            // Option 1: Return error (stops workflow)
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to process item %d: %w", i, err)

            // Option 2: Continue with error data
            results = append(results, model.DataItem{
                JSON: map[string]interface{}{
                    "error": err.Error(),
                    "item":  item.JSON,
                },
            })
            continue
        }

        results = append(results, result)
    }

    return results, nil
}

docs.title: Testing Nodes
docs.text:
Write comprehensive tests:

package custom

import (
    "testing"

    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    "github.com/yourusername/m9m/internal/model"
)

func TestMyCustomNode_Execute(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        input     []model.DataItem
        params    map[string]interface{}
        expected  []model.DataItem
        expectErr bool
    }{
        {
            name: "basic processing",
            input: []model.DataItem{
                {JSON: map[string]interface{}{"value": 1}},
            },
            params: map[string]interface{}{
                "message": "test",
            },
            expected: []model.DataItem{
                {JSON: map[string]interface{}{"value": 1, "processed": true}},
            },
            expectErr: false,
        },
        {
            name:      "empty input",
            input:     []model.DataItem{},
            params:    map[string]interface{}{},
            expected:  []model.DataItem{},
            expectErr: false,
        },
    }

    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            node := NewMyCustomNode()

            result, err := node.Execute(tt.input, tt.params)

            if tt.expectErr {
                assert.Error(t, err)
                return
            }

            require.NoError(t, err)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}

func TestMyCustomNode_ValidateParameters(t *testing.T) {
    node := NewMyCustomNode()

    err := node.ValidateParameters(nil)
    assert.NoError(t, err)

    err = node.ValidateParameters(map[string]interface{}{
        "message": "valid",
    })
    assert.NoError(t, err)
}

docs.title: Transaction Pattern
docs.text:
Use code node for transactions:

const client = await pool.connect();
try {
  await client.query('BEGIN');
  await client.query('INSERT INTO orders ...');
  await client.query('UPDATE inventory ...');
  await client.query('COMMIT');
} catch (e) {
  await client.query('ROLLBACK');
  throw e;
} finally {
  client.release();
}

  maxConnections: 20

docs.title: Handle Not Found
docs.text:
if ($json.length === 0) {

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use parameterized queries - Never concatenate user input
  2. Least privilege access - Use read-only users when possible
  3. Encrypt connections - Enable SSL/TLS
  4. Rotate credentials - Change passwords regularly
  5. Audit queries - Log database operations

docs.title: Performance Tips
docs.text:
1. Index frequently queried columns
  2. Use LIMIT for large result sets
  3. Batch inserts for multiple records
  4. Use connection pooling
  5. Cache read-heavy queries with Redis

  • Error Handling - Handle database errors

docs.title: Cursor-Based
docs.text:
// In Code node
let allItems = [];
let cursor = null;

do {
  const url = cursor
    ? `https://api.example.com/items?cursor=${cursor}`
    : 'https://api.example.com/items';

  const response = await fetch(url);
  const data = await response.json();

  allItems = allItems.concat(data.items);
  cursor = data.nextCursor;
} while (cursor);

return allItems.map(item => ({ json: item }));

docs.title: Nodes Overview
docs.text: Nodes are the building blocks of m9m workflows. Each node performs a specific action or transformation.

docs.title: Node Categories
docs.text: Category Description Examples Triggers Start workflow execution Webhook, Cron, Start Transform Manipulate data Set, Filter, Merge, Code HTTP/API Make HTTP requests HTTP Request, GraphQL Database Database operations PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB Messaging Send messages Slack, Discord, Email AI AI/LLM operations OpenAI, Anthropic Cloud Cloud services AWS S3, GCP Storage VCS Version control GitHub, GitLab

docs.title: Node Structure
docs.text:
Every node has:

{
  "id": "unique-node-id",
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.nodeType",
  "position": [x, y],
  "parameters": {},
  "credentials": {}
}

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Description id Unique identifier within the workflow type Node type (e.g., n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) position Visual position in editor [x, y] parameters Configuration options credentials Authentication reference

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
Nodes process data as items. Each item contains:

{
  "json": {
    "field1": "value1",
    "field2": "value2"
  },
  "binary": {
    "data": "<base64-encoded>"
  }
}

docs.title: Input and Output
docs.text:
• Nodes receive items from connected upstream nodes
  • Process each item according to their configuration
  • Output items to connected downstream nodes
[Input Items] → [Node Processing] → [Output Items]

docs.title: Multiple Items
docs.text:
Most nodes process each input item independently:

Input: [item1, item2, item3]
       ↓       ↓       ↓
      [Node Processing]
       ↓       ↓       ↓
Output: [result1, result2, result3]

docs.title: All Nodes
docs.text: Parameter Description continueOnFail Continue workflow on error retryOnFail Retry on failure maxRetries Maximum retry attempts

Most parameters support expressions:

    "url": "={{ $env.API_URL }}/users/{{ $json.userId }}"

docs.title: Multiple Triggers
docs.text:
A workflow can have multiple triggers:

{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "cron-trigger",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.cron",
      "parameters": {
        "triggerTimes": {"item": [{"mode": "everyHour"}]}
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "webhook-trigger",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
      "parameters": {
        "path": "manual-run",
        "httpMethod": "POST"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "process",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set"
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "cron-trigger": {
      "main": [[{"node": "process", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    },
    "webhook-trigger": {
      "main": [[{"node": "process", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Activate/Deactivate
docs.text:
Triggers only run when the workflow is active:

# Activate workflow
m9m workflow activate wf-001

# Deactivate
m9m workflow deactivate wf-001

docs.title: Test Triggers
docs.text:
Test without activating:

m9m execute workflow.json --trigger-data '{"body": {"test": true}}'

docs.title: HMAC Signature
docs.text:
Verify webhook signatures:

{
  "parameters": {
    "path": "secure",
    "httpMethod": "POST",
    "authentication": "hmacSignature",
    "signatureHeader": "X-Hub-Signature-256",
    "algorithm": "sha256"
  }
}

docs.title: IP Whitelist
docs.text:
Configure in server settings:

webhooks:
  allowedIps:
    - "192.168.1.0/24"
    - "10.0.0.1"

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
webhooks:
  rateLimit:
    requests: 100
    period: "1m"

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use meaningful webhook paths - /orders/new not /webhook1
  2. Secure webhooks with authentication
  3. Set appropriate cron intervals - don't poll too frequently
  4. Handle duplicate events - webhooks may retry
  5. Log trigger events for debugging

docs.title: serve
docs.text:
Start the m9m server.

m9m serve [options]
Option Default Description --port, -p 8080 HTTP port --host 0.0.0.0 Bind address --metrics-port 9090 Metrics port --config, -c config.yaml Config file

# Start with defaults

docs.title: execute
docs.text:

m9m execute [options] <workflow-file>
Option Description --input, -i Input data (JSON) --input-file Input data file --start-node Start from specific node --skip-trigger Skip trigger nodes --output, -o Output file --format Output format (json, table)

Examples: 

# Execute workflow
m9m execute workflow.json

m9m execute workflow.json --input '{"key": "value"}'

m9m execute workflow.json --input-file data.json

# Start from specific node
m9m execute workflow.json --start-node "process-data"

m9m execute workflow.json --output result.json

docs.title: Common Workflows

# Start server and run workflow
m9m serve &
m9m execute my-workflow.json

# Deploy workflow from CI/CD
m9m workflow import production-workflow.json
m9m workflow activate <id>

docs.title: Scripting
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash
# Execute workflow and check result

RESULT=$(m9m execute workflow.json --format json)
STATUS=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.status')

if [ "$STATUS" = "success" ]; then
    echo "Workflow completed successfully"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Workflow failed: $(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.error')"
    exit 1
fi

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all m9m configuration options.

docs.title: Configuration Methods
docs.text:
Configuration can be set via:

  1. Configuration file (config.yaml)
  2. Environment variables (prefix: M9M_)
  3. Command-line flags

Priority: CLI flags > Environment variables > Config file > Defaults

docs.title: Core Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description server.port M9M_PORT 8080 HTTP server port server.host M9M_HOST 0.0.0.0 Server bind address server.baseUrl M9M_BASE_URL http://localhost:8080 Public URL 
server:
  port: 8080
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  baseUrl: "https://m9m.example.com"

docs.title: HTTP Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description server.readTimeout M9M_READ_TIMEOUT 30s Request read timeout server.writeTimeout M9M_WRITE_TIMEOUT 30s Response write timeout server.idleTimeout M9M_IDLE_TIMEOUT 120s Keep-alive timeout server.maxHeaderBytes M9M_MAX_HEADER_BYTES 1MB Max header size 
server:
  readTimeout: 30s
  writeTimeout: 30s
  idleTimeout: 120s
  maxHeaderBytes: 1048576

docs.title: Connection Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description database.type M9M_DB_TYPE sqlite Database type database.url M9M_DB_URL file:m9m.db Connection URL

Supported types: sqlite, postgres, mysql

database:
  type: postgres
  url: "postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/m9m?sslmode=require"

docs.title: Pool Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description database.maxConnections M9M_DB_MAX_CONNS 25 Max open connections database.maxIdleConnections M9M_DB_MAX_IDLE 5 Max idle connections database.connectionTimeout M9M_DB_CONN_TIMEOUT 5s Connection timeout database.connMaxLifetime M9M_DB_CONN_LIFETIME 1h Max connection age 
database:
  maxConnections: 50
  maxIdleConnections: 10
  connectionTimeout: 5s
  connMaxLifetime: 1h

docs.title: Queue Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description queue.type M9M_QUEUE_TYPE memory Queue backend queue.url M9M_QUEUE_URL - Queue connection URL queue.maxWorkers M9M_MAX_WORKERS 10 Concurrent workers

Supported types: memory, redis, rabbitmq

queue:
  type: redis
  url: "redis://:password@localhost:6379/0"
  maxWorkers: 20

docs.title: Worker Settings
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description queue.batchSize M9M_BATCH_SIZE 10 Items per batch queue.pollInterval M9M_POLL_INTERVAL 100ms Queue poll frequency queue.visibilityTimeout M9M_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT 30s Task lock timeout queue.retryDelay M9M_RETRY_DELAY 1s Retry backoff queue.maxRetries M9M_MAX_RETRIES 3 Max retry attempts 
queue:
  batchSize: 10
  pollInterval: 100ms
  visibilityTimeout: 30s
  retryDelay: 1s
  maxRetries: 3

docs.title: Encryption
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description security.encryptionKey M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY - AES encryption key (32 chars) 
security:
  encryptionKey: "your-32-character-encryption-key"

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description auth.enabled M9M_AUTH_ENABLED false Enable authentication auth.apiKeys.enabled M9M_API_KEYS_ENABLED true Enable API key auth 
auth:
  enabled: true
  apiKeys:
    enabled: true
  jwt:
    enabled: true
    secret: "your-jwt-secret"
    accessTokenExpiry: 1h
    refreshTokenExpiry: 7d

docs.title: CORS
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description security.cors.enabled M9M_CORS_ENABLED false Enable CORS security.cors.origins M9M_CORS_ORIGINS * Allowed origins 
security:
  cors:
    enabled: true
    origins:
      - "https://app.example.com"
    methods: ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]
    headers: ["Authorization", "Content-Type"]
    credentials: true

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description security.rateLimit.enabled M9M_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED false Enable rate limiting security.rateLimit.requests M9M_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS 100 Requests per period security.rateLimit.period M9M_RATE_LIMIT_PERIOD 1m Rate limit period 
security:
  rateLimit:
    enabled: true
    requests: 100
    period: 1m
    byApiKey: true

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description monitoring.metricsEnabled M9M_METRICS_ENABLED true Enable Prometheus metrics monitoring.metricsPort M9M_METRICS_PORT 9090 Metrics server port monitoring.metricsPath M9M_METRICS_PATH /metrics Metrics endpoint 
monitoring:
  metricsEnabled: true
  metricsPort: 9090
  metricsPath: /metrics

docs.title: Tracing
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description monitoring.tracingEnabled M9M_TRACING_ENABLED false Enable tracing monitoring.tracingEndpoint M9M_TRACING_ENDPOINT - Jaeger/OTLP endpoint monitoring.serviceName M9M_SERVICE_NAME m9m Service name for tracing 
monitoring:
  tracingEnabled: true
  tracingEndpoint: "http://jaeger:14268/api/traces"
  serviceName: "m9m"
  sampleRate: 0.1

docs.title: Logging Configuration
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description logging.level M9M_LOG_LEVEL info Log level logging.format M9M_LOG_FORMAT text Log format logging.output M9M_LOG_OUTPUT stdout Log output

Log levels: debug, info, warn, error Formats: text, json

logging:
  level: info
  format: json
  output: stdout
  fields:
    environment: production
    version: "1.0.0"

docs.title: Execution Configuration
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description execution.timeout M9M_EXECUTION_TIMEOUT 300s Default workflow timeout execution.saveSuccessData M9M_SAVE_SUCCESS_DATA all Save successful executions execution.saveErrorData M9M_SAVE_ERROR_DATA all Save failed executions execution.pruneAfter M9M_PRUNE_AFTER 30d Execution retention 
execution:
  timeout: 300s
  saveSuccessData: all  # all, none, summary
  saveErrorData: all
  pruneAfter: 30d

docs.title: Webhooks Configuration
docs.text:
Option Env Var Default Description webhooks.timeout M9M_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT 30s Webhook timeout webhooks.maxBodySize M9M_WEBHOOK_MAX_BODY 10MB Max request body webhooks.path M9M_WEBHOOK_PATH /webhook Webhook base path 
webhooks:
  timeout: 30s
  maxBodySize: 10485760  # 10MB
  path: /webhook
  allowedIps:
    - "0.0.0.0/0"

docs.title: SMTP
docs.text:
smtp:
  host: "smtp.example.com"
  port: 587
  user: "notifications@example.com"
  password: "${SMTP_PASSWORD}"
  from: "m9m <notifications@example.com>"
  tls: true

docs.title: S3 Storage
docs.text:
storage:
  type: s3
  bucket: "m9m-storage"
  region: "us-east-1"
  accessKeyId: "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"
  secretAccessKey: "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
# config.yaml
server:
  port: 8080
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  baseUrl: "https://m9m.example.com"
  readTimeout: 30s
  writeTimeout: 30s

database:
  type: postgres
  url: "${DATABASE_URL}"
  maxConnections: 50
  maxIdleConnections: 10

queue:
  type: redis
  url: "${REDIS_URL}"
  maxWorkers: 20
  batchSize: 10

security:
  encryptionKey: "${ENCRYPTION_KEY}"
  cors:
    enabled: true
    origins:
      - "https://app.example.com"
  rateLimit:
    enabled: true
    requests: 1000
    period: 1m

auth:
  enabled: true
  jwt:
    secret: "${JWT_SECRET}"
    accessTokenExpiry: 1h

monitoring:
  metricsEnabled: true
  metricsPort: 9090
  tracingEnabled: true
  tracingEndpoint: "http://jaeger:14268/api/traces"

logging:
  level: info
  format: json

execution:
  timeout: 300s
  saveSuccessData: summary
  pruneAfter: 30d

webhooks:
  timeout: 30s
  maxBodySize: 10485760

docs.title: Environment Variable Reference
docs.text:
All config options can be set via environment variables using the M9M_ prefix and snake_case:

# Server
export M9M_PORT=8080
export M9M_HOST=0.0.0.0

# Database
export M9M_DB_TYPE=postgres
export M9M_DB_URL="postgres://..."

# Queue
export M9M_QUEUE_TYPE=redis
export M9M_QUEUE_URL="redis://..."
export M9M_MAX_WORKERS=20

# Security
export M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY="..."
export M9M_AUTH_ENABLED=true

# Monitoring
export M9M_METRICS_ENABLED=true
export M9M_LOG_LEVEL=info

docs.title: Migration from n8n
docs.text: Migrate your workflows and data from n8n to m9m.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
m9m provides 95% compatibility with n8n:

Feature Compatibility Workflows Full Expressions Full Nodes 95%+ Credentials Full Webhooks Full Variables Full

docs.title: Export Workflows
docs.text:
# Export all workflows
curl -X GET http://n8n-host:5678/api/v1/workflows \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key" \
  -o workflows.json

# Export specific workflow
curl -X GET http://n8n-host:5678/api/v1/workflows/<id> \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key" \
  -o workflow.json

Or via n8n UI: 1. Go to Workflows 2. Select workflow → ... → Download

docs.title: Export Credentials
docs.text:
curl -X GET http://n8n-host:5678/api/v1/credentials \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key" \
  -o credentials.json

Credential Security

Credential secrets are not exported by default. You'll need to re-enter secrets in m9m.

docs.title: 2. Prepare m9m
docs.text:
# Start m9m
m9m serve

# Or with Docker
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 m9m/m9m:latest

docs.title: 3. Import Workflows
docs.text:
# Import single workflow
m9m workflow import workflow.json

# Import multiple workflows
for f in workflows/*.json; do
  m9m workflow import "$f"
done

Or via API: 

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @workflow.json

docs.title: 4. Configure Credentials
docs.text:
Re-create credentials with your secrets:

m9m credentials create "Slack Bot" \
  --type slackApi \
  --data '{"accessToken": "xoxb-your-token"}'

docs.title: 5. Test Workflows
docs.text:
# Validate workflow
m9m validate workflow.json

# Test execution
m9m execute workflow.json --skip-trigger

docs.title: 6. Activate Workflows
docs.text: m9m workflow activate <id>

docs.title: Fully Compatible Nodes
docs.text:
• Core: Start, Set, IF, Switch, Merge, Split In Batches
  • Transform: Filter, Sort, Rename Keys, Item Lists
  • HTTP: HTTP Request, Webhook
  • Code: Code (JavaScript), Function
  • Database: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis
  • Messaging: Slack, Discord, Email (SMTP)
  • Cloud: AWS S3, AWS Lambda, GCP Storage
  • AI: OpenAI, Anthropic

docs.title: Nodes with Differences
docs.text: Node Compatibility Notes Execute Command Partial Different execution environment SSH Partial Requires additional setup FTP Partial Use SFTP for better support

docs.title: Unsupported Nodes
docs.text:
Some specialized n8n nodes may not be available. Use alternatives:

n8n Node m9m Alternative n8n-nodes-custom Create custom Go node

docs.title: Expression Migration
docs.text:
Expressions are fully compatible:

// These work identically in m9m
{{ $json.field }}
{{ $input.first().json.data }}
{{ $node["HTTP Request"].json.statusCode }}
{{ $now.format('YYYY-MM-DD') }}

docs.title: Automatic Migration
docs.text:
m9m migrate-credentials \
  --from-n8n http://n8n-host:5678 \
  --api-key your-n8n-api-key

This imports credential metadata. Secrets must be re-entered.

docs.title: Manual Migration
docs.text:
1. List n8n credentials
  2. Create equivalent in m9m
  3. Update workflows to reference new credential IDs

docs.title: Webhook Migration
docs.text:
Webhook URLs change format:

n8n m9m n8n-host:5678/webhook/<path> m9m-host:8080/webhook/<path> n8n-host:5678/webhook-test/<path> m9m-host:8080/webhook-test/<path>

Update your integrations with new URLs.

docs.title: Execution History
docs.text:
Export execution history if needed:

# Export from n8n
curl -X GET "http://n8n-host:5678/api/v1/executions?limit=1000" \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key" \
  -o executions.json

# Import to m9m (optional)
m9m executions import executions.json

docs.title: Workflow Data
docs.text:
Workflow definitions are fully compatible:

# Direct import works
m9m workflow import n8n-workflow.json

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: n8n m9m N8N_PORT M9M_PORT N8N_HOST M9M_HOST DB_TYPE M9M_DB_TYPE DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST M9M_DB_URL EXECUTIONS_DATA_SAVE_ON_SUCCESS M9M_SAVE_SUCCESS_DATA QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_HOST M9M_QUEUE_URL N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY M9M_ENCRYPTION_KEY

docs.title: Queue Migration
docs.text: n8n m9m Bull (Redis) Redis - RabbitMQ (additional option)

docs.title: Validation Script
docs.text:
Use this script to validate migration:

#!/bin/bash
# migration-validate.sh

echo "Validating m9m migration..."

# Check workflows
echo "Checking workflows..."
WORKFLOWS=$(m9m workflow list --format json | jq length)
echo "  Imported workflows: $WORKFLOWS"

# Check credentials
echo "Checking credentials..."
CREDS=$(m9m credentials list --format json | jq length)
echo "  Imported credentials: $CREDS"

# Test sample workflow
echo "Testing sample workflow..."
m9m execute test-workflow.json --skip-trigger
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  Sample workflow: PASSED"
else
    echo "  Sample workflow: FAILED"
fi

# Check webhooks
echo "Checking webhook endpoints..."
curl -s http://localhost:8080/webhook/test > /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  Webhooks: ACTIVE"
else
    echo "  Webhooks: INACTIVE"
fi

echo "Migration validation complete."

docs.title: Rollback Plan
docs.text:
If migration fails:

  1. Keep n8n running during migration
  2. Test m9m thoroughly before switching
  3. Use DNS/load balancer to switch traffic
  4. Keep n8n available for 30 days as fallback
# Quick rollback
# 1. Update DNS/load balancer to point to n8n
# 2. Deactivate m9m workflows
m9m workflow list | xargs -I {} m9m workflow deactivate {}

docs.title: Performance Comparison
docs.text:
After migration, you should see:

Metric Improvement Startup time 6x faster Execution speed 5-10x faster Memory usage 70% less Container size 75% smaller

docs.title: Workflow Import Fails
docs.text:
# Validate JSON
jq . workflow.json

# Check for unsupported nodes
m9m validate workflow.json

docs.title: Credentials Not Working
docs.text:
1. Verify credential type matches
  2. Re-enter secrets
  3. Test credential connection

docs.title: Expression Errors
docs.text:
1. Check for n8n-specific functions
  2. Verify variable references
  3. Test expressions individually

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and solutions for m9m.

docs.title: Quick Diagnostics
docs.text:
# Check server health
curl http://localhost:8080/health

# Check readiness
curl http://localhost:8080/ready

# View logs
m9m logs --tail 100

# Check version
m9m version

docs.title: Server Won't Start
docs.text:
Symptom: Server exits immediately or hangs.

Solutions:

  1. Port already in use 

    # Check what's using the port
lsof -i :8080

# Use different port
m9m serve --port 3000

  2. Database connection failed 

    # Test database connection
psql "$M9M_DB_URL" -c "SELECT 1"

# Check connection string
echo $M9M_DB_URL

  3. Missing configuration 

    # Verify config file
m9m serve --config /path/to/config.yaml

docs.title: Database Errors
docs.text:
Symptom: connection refused or authentication failed

Solutions:

  1. Verify database is running: 

    docker ps | grep postgres

  2. Check connection URL format: 

    postgres://user:password@host:5432/database?sslmode=disable

  3. Verify credentials: 

    psql -h localhost -U m9m -d m9m

docs.title: Queue Connection Failed
docs.text:
Symptom: redis: connection refused

Solutions:

  1. Check Redis is running: 

    redis-cli ping

  2. Verify URL format: 

    redis://:password@localhost:6379/0

docs.title: Workflow Timeout
docs.text:
Symptom: execution timeout exceeded

Solutions:

  1. Increase timeout: 

    execution:
  timeout: 600s

  2. Check for infinite loops in workflow

  3. Optimize slow nodes (add caching, reduce data)

docs.title: Node Execution Failed
docs.text:
Symptom: Specific node fails with error

Solutions:

  1. HTTP Request errors 

    Enable retry:
retryOnFail: true
maxRetries: 3

  2. Credential errors 

    # Test credential
m9m credentials test <id>

  3. Expression errors

  4. Check for undefined variables
  5. Use optional chaining: $json.field?.nested

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
Symptom: out of memory or process killed

Solutions:

  1. Increase memory limit: 

    resources:
  limits:
    memory: 2Gi

  2. Process data in batches: 

    {
  "type": "n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches",
  "parameters": {"batchSize": 100}
}

  3. Clear large data between nodes

docs.title: Webhook Not Responding
docs.text:
Symptom: 404 or no response from webhook

Solutions:

  1. Verify workflow is active: 

    m9m workflow list | grep <name>

  2. Check webhook path: 

    curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/webhook/your-path

  3. Check logs for errors: 

    m9m logs --filter webhook

docs.title: Webhook Timeout
docs.text:
Symptom: Gateway timeout (504)

Solutions:

  1. Use onReceived response mode: 

    {
  "parameters": {
    "responseMode": "onReceived",
    "responseCode": 202
  }
}

  2. Process asynchronously via queue

docs.title: Slow Workflow Execution
docs.text:
Symptom: Workflows take longer than expected

Solutions:

  1. Enable profiling: 

    M9M_ENABLE_PPROF=true m9m serve

  2. Check bottlenecks: 

    curl http://localhost:8080/debug/pprof/profile?seconds=30 > profile.out
go tool pprof profile.out

  3. Optimize:

  4. Cache HTTP responses
  5. Reduce data transformations
  6. Use batch operations

docs.title: High Memory Usage
docs.text:
Symptom: Memory grows over time

Solutions:

  1. Check for memory leaks: 

    curl http://localhost:8080/debug/pprof/heap > heap.out
go tool pprof heap.out

  2. Clear execution history: 

    m9m executions prune --older-than 7d

  3. Reduce saved execution data: 

    execution:
  saveSuccessData: summary

docs.title: Queue Backlog
docs.text:
Symptom: Queue depth growing

Solutions:

  1. Increase workers: 

    queue:
  maxWorkers: 50

  2. Scale horizontally: 

    kubectl scale deployment/m9m-workers --replicas=5

  3. Check for failing workflows blocking queue

docs.title: Database Connection Pool Exhausted
docs.text:
Symptom: too many connections

Solutions:

  1. Increase pool size: 

    database:
  maxConnections: 100

  2. Use connection pooler (PgBouncer): 

    database:
  url: "postgres://pgbouncer:6432/m9m"

  3. Check for connection leaks

docs.title: Redis Connection Errors
docs.text:
Symptom: connection reset by peer

Solutions:

  1. Increase timeout: 

    queue:
  connectionTimeout: 10s

  2. Check Redis memory: 

    redis-cli info memory

  3. Enable keepalive: 

    queue:
  keepAlive: true

docs.title: API Key Invalid
docs.text:
Symptom: 401 Unauthorized

Solutions:

  1. Verify key format: 

    curl -H "X-API-Key: m9m_sk_..." http://localhost:8080/api/v1/workflows

  2. Check key exists: 

    m9m apikey list

  3. Regenerate key: 

    m9m apikey create --name "New Key"

docs.title: Token Expired
docs.text:
Symptom: 401 Token expired

Solutions:

  1. Refresh token: 

    curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/refresh \
  -d '{"refreshToken": "..."}'

  2. Increase token expiry: 

    auth:
  jwt:
    accessTokenExpiry: 24h

docs.title: Enable Debug Logging
docs.text: M9M_LOG_LEVEL=debug m9m serve

docs.title: Log to File
docs.text:
logging:
  output: /var/log/m9m/m9m.log

docs.title: Filter Logs
docs.text:
# Workflow errors only
m9m logs --filter error --workflow <id>

# Specific node
m9m logs --filter "node=HTTP Request"

docs.title: Gather Diagnostics
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash
# diagnostics.sh

echo "=== m9m Diagnostics ==="
echo ""
echo "Version:"
m9m version
echo ""
echo "Health:"
curl -s http://localhost:8080/health | jq
echo ""
echo "Config:"
m9m config show
echo ""
echo "Recent Errors:"
m9m logs --filter error --limit 20
echo ""
echo "System Resources:"
free -h
df -h
echo ""
echo "Processes:"
ps aux | grep m9m

docs.title: Report Issues
docs.text:
Include in bug reports:

  1. m9m version (m9m version)
  2. Configuration (sanitized)
  3. Error logs
  4. Steps to reproduce
  5. Expected vs actual behavior

Submit issues at: https://github.com/m9m/m9m/issues
